steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.iudicare: 'iudicare' is an infinitive (to judge), not a finite verb
hints.noli: 'noli' is the singular imperative — 'Nolite' is the plural imperative used here
hints.iudicate: 'iudicate' means 'judge!' but does not appear in this phrase

steps.target: to be unwilling / to not want
choices: to be unwilling / to not want

hints.to know: 'to know' is 'noscere' — 'Nolite' comes from 'nolle' (to be unwilling)
hints.to harm: 'to harm' is 'nocere' — 'Nolite' comes from 'nolle' (to be unwilling)
hints.to refuse: 'to refuse' is 'recusare' — 'nolle' means 'to be unwilling' (noli + inf. = prohibition)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 2nd plural is 'non vultis' — 'Nolite' is imperative
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'nolitis' — 'Nolite' is the imperative plural
hints.infinitive: Infinitive is 'nolle' — 'Nolite' is the imperative plural form

hints.nosco, noscere, novi, notum: These are principal parts of 'nosco' (to get to know)
hints.noceo, nocere, nocui, nocitum: These are principal parts of 'noceo' (to harm)
hints.volo, velle, volui, —: These are principal parts of 'volo' (to wish) — 'nolo' is its negative compound

hints.imperative: Imperative would be 'iudica/iudicate' — 'iudicare' ending in -are is the infinitive
hints.gerund: Gerund would be 'iudicandi' — 'iudicare' is the present active infinitive
hints.participle: Participle would be 'iudicans' — 'iudicare' is the infinitive

hints.iuro, iurare, iuravi, iuratum: These are principal parts of 'iuro' (to swear an oath)
hints.indico, indicare, indicavi, indicatum: These are principal parts of 'indico' (to indicate/point out)
hints.iuvo, iuvare, iuvi, iutum: These are principal parts of 'iuvo' (to help)
